meta.executive_summary: TestProject is a small test-scope application demonstrating a layered MVC architecture with a repository pattern for data access. The primary flow is user authentication: a client calls AuthController, which delegates to UserService, which reads/writes via UserRepository and PostgreSQL. Sessions use stateless JWT. This fixture covers every blueprint field the LLM Wiki renders.
meta.architecture_style: Layered MVC with repository pattern. HTTP controllers remain thin; UserService owns business logic; UserRepository abstracts storage. Auth is stateless via JWT to allow horizontal scaling.

architectural_style.rationale: Separation of concerns + testable data layer. Matches team experience.

trade_offs.accepted_cost: Token revocation is harder with stateless JWT
Schema migrations require care in CI
trade_offs.gained_benefit: Strong relational consistency for financial data

components.name: UserService
components.responsibility: Owns user authentication and session lifecycle. Validates credentials via UserRepository, issues JWTs, handles password hashing with bcrypt, and exposes login/logout/signup operations. All methods are async and return Result<T, AuthError>.

key_interfaces.name: login
key_interfaces.signature: async login(email: string, password: string): Promise<Result<Session, AuthError>>
key_interfaces.description: Validates credentials and issues a new JWT session.

key_interfaces.name: signup
key_interfaces.signature: async signup(email: string, password: string): Promise<Result<User, AuthError>>
key_interfaces.description: Creates a new user after password validation.

components.name: UserRepository
components.responsibility: Thin data-access wrapper over PostgreSQL. Never contains business logic. Exposes typed CRUD for User records and hashed-password comparisons.

components.name: AuthController
components.responsibility: HTTP boundary for authentication. Maps HTTP requests to UserService calls and serializes Result types into HTTP responses.

patterns.name: Repository
patterns.when_to_use: When abstracting a data source behind a contract
patterns.when_not_to_use: For trivial single-table CRUD without future flexibility needs

pattern_selection_guide.scenario: Need to add a new HTTP endpoint

pattern_selection_guide.recommended_pattern: Go through a Repository, never direct SQL from Service

pitfalls.recommendation: Move auth logic out of lib

capabilities.name: User Authentication
capabilities.purpose: Users sign up, log in, and receive a JWT.

file_placement_rules.rationale: Feature-colocated; keeps HTTP surface next to business logic.
Data access is always isolated from services.

implementation_guidelines.name: How to add a new auth-protected endpoint
implementation_guidelines.pattern_description: Define the route in the Controller. Delegate to a Service method. Service calls Repository. All return Result<T, E>.
tips: Never call UserRepository from a Controller directly.

implementation_guidelines.name: How to hash a new password
implementation_guidelines.pattern_description: Use bcrypt with cost factor 12. Never store plaintext.
implementation_guidelines.usage_example:
import bcrypt from 'bcrypt';
const hash = await bcrypt.hash(password, 12);
tips: Cost factor 12 is the current team standard.

development_rules.rule: Never log passwords or password hashes, even at DEBUG level.
development_rules.rationale: Logs get aggregated; leak risk is unacceptable.

development_rules.rule: All password comparisons use bcrypt.compare, never string equality.
Services never touch the database directly; always go through a Repository.
development_rules.rationale: Keeps tests fast and the dependency graph one-directional.

development_rules.rule: Every public async method returns a Result<T, E>, never throws.
development_rules.rationale: Uniform error surface across the codebase.

technology.project_structure: Monorepo with features/ as the primary unit. Each feature contains its Controller, Service, Repository, and tests.

pattern_selection.pattern: requireAuth middleware + Service.method that accepts userId

error_mapping.solution: Return JSON with code 'invalid_credentials'; log event (without password)
Return JSON with code 'user_exists'; client should prompt login

frontend.conventions: Page components in app/; client components explicitly marked 'use client'. Shared UI in components/ui/.

architecture_diagram:
graph TD
  AuthController[AuthController] --> UserService[UserService]
  UserService --> UserRepository[UserRepository]
  UserRepository --> PostgreSQL[(PostgreSQL)]
